title: Teaching Techniques with Special Needs Children
description: Teaching experiences. A retarded Indian boy. Teaching techniques. Developing rapport with youngsters and the community. Children's handicaps.

title: Interaction Between Teachers and the Community; Get-togethers at School
description: Teachers were at the center of the community. Get-togethers at school. A teacher could easily offend the culture of the community. Need to bring food for children.

title: Produce brought to the Valley
description: Hot dandelion salad for a visitor to the homestead. Himalayas blackberries and walnut trees were brought to the valley by the Grosecloses, and have since taken over.

title: Role of a Teacher
description: Role of teacher - her difficulties, and the challenge of providing advice.

title: Rural Schools; School Library; Do-It-Yourself Manuals
description: Printed matter for the rural schools. The school library; apportioning purchases. Do-it-yourself manuals. Need to improve the water supply and the handling of milk. Where she turned for help; importance of outside sources of information.

title: Oxen Used in Logging; Stopping Milk from Souring
description: Using oxen in logging. A cabinet to stop milk from souring.

title: Country Women Longing for a Better Life; Natural Medicine
description: The longing of country women for a better life. Use of natural plants in preventive medicine and for curing. Jerusalem artichokes, yarrow, shinnebark, Virginians particularly used them.

title: In-Laws
description: Her mother-in-law's Bohemian parents. Grandmother used camphor and hops.

title: Having Babies at Home; Neighbor's Miscarriage
description: Having babies at home with help from neighbors. A neighbor's miscarriage.

title: Male and Female Life; Arranged Marriage
description: Occasionally men taught; they got more money because they were "the breadwinners." An adage for spendthrift women. Pioneer women had a heavy burden; she really began to work when her mother was ill. Importance of teaching as an avenue of self-expression. Women want homes and families; a homemaker should make the home comfortable. Her mother-in-law's strictness compared with her own sense of security. She's been a "free woman" because she's done what she's wanted to. Some women aren't free: an arranged marriage.

title: Raleigh's Fine Qualities; Wanting to Marry Raleigh
description: She was engaged to a doctor who was a fibber, which was totally unacceptable to her. Telling her parents that she wanted to marry Raleigh, a vegetable pedlar. Going on the vegetable route with Raleigh: he worries about giving a woman one nickel too little in change. His fine qualities. Other men asked him why he gave his daughters checking accounts.

title: Childbearing Tragedy
description: Giving birth to a blue baby. His funeral, to have more children.
